mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate, even if stable, indicates slack in the labor market which limits wage inflation pressures and allows for accommodative monetary policy, supporting equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.1% as of t-1mo is historically elevated
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently produces negative 60-day returns when UNRATE is above 7% and stable, or if the Rank IC for UNRATE is consistently negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over the short term.
failure_condition: If, after a trigger, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time, or if the average return is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it signals an environment of ample liquidity and low cost of capital, which is highly supportive of equity valuations as discount rates remain suppressed and real returns are attractive.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% and CPIAUCSL at 229.177 (t-1mo) suggest low rates and moderate inflation
failure_condition: If, under these joint conditions, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly negative or show no significant outperformance compared to periods outside this regime (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts during an economic downturn signals a deepening recession, which negatively impacts consumer confidence and future economic activity, leading to further declines in equity prices.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declines by more than 10% from its 6-month peak
failure_condition: If, during periods identified as recessionary, a decline in HOUST does not precede or coincide with negative S&P 500 returns, or if SPX returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production indicates a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which directly impacts corporate earnings and signals a deteriorating economic outlook, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate falls below -3%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances where this trigger is met, or if the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent, moderate growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, providing a stable foundation for positive equity market performance over the medium term.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS growth and S&P 500 60-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in both real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based economic strength and robust consumer and business demand, which is a powerful driver for corporate earnings growth and thus positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4%
failure_condition: If, when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show strong growth, the S&P 500 20-day returns are negative more than 45% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, a rising federal funds rate increases the cost of capital and reduces liquidity, making equities less attractive relative to fixed income and leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: If, during a clear monetary tightening cycle, a FEDFUNDS hike does not lead to negative S&P 500 5-day returns, or if the average return is positive in > 50% of triggered instances.
